briefdescription.para: The pixels of the own drawable and the mask drawable are alpha blended. As a result, only the part of the own drawable, which intersects with the mask drawable is visible.
The pixels of the own drawable and the complement to the mask drawable's pixels are alpha blended. As a result, only the part of the own which is not covered by the mask is visible.
Enumeration indicating the type used in the masking of two objects - the mask drawable and the own drawable.
The remaining area is filled with the closest stop color.
The gradient pattern is reflected outside the gradient area until the expected region is filled.
The gradient pattern is repeated continuously beyond the gradient area until the expected region is filled.
Enumeration specifying how to fill the area outside the gradient bounds.
The end of lines is rendered as a square around the last point.
The end of lines is rendered as a half-circle around the last point.
The end of lines is rendered as a full stop on the last point itself.
Enumeration for The cap style to be used for stroking the path.
Used to render beveled line joins. The outer corner of the joined lines is filled by enclosing the triangular region of the corner with a straight line between the outer corners of each stroke.
Used to render rounded line joins. Circular arcs are used to join two lines smoothly.

Used to render mitered line joins. The intersection of the strokes is clipped at a line perpendicular to the bisector of the angle between the strokes, at the distance from the intersection of the segments equal to the product of the miter limit value and the border radius. This prevents long spikes being created.
Enumeration for The join style to be used for stroking the path.

Draw a horizontal line from the point to a location outside the shape. Determine whether the direction of the line at each intersection point is up or down. The winding number is determined by summing the direction of each intersection. If the number is non zero, the point is inside the shape.
Draw a horizontal line from the point to a location outside the shape, and count the number of intersections. If the number of intersections is an odd number, the point is inside the shape.
Enumeration for The fill rule of shape.
Ends the current sub-path and connects it with its initial point. This command doesn't expect any points.
Sets a new initial point of the sub-path and a new current point. This command expects 1 point: the starting position.
Draws a line from the current point to the given point and sets a new value of the current point. This command expects 1 point: the end-position of the line.

Draws a cubic Bezier curve from the current point to the given point using two given control points and sets a new value of the current point. This command expects 3 points: the 1st control-point, the 2nd control-point, the end-point of the curve.
Enumeration specifying the values of the path commands. Not to be confused with the path commands from the svg path element (like M, L, Q, H and many others).
List of Colorstop.
Creates an initialized handle to a new CanvasRenderer::DrawableGroup.

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated DrawableGroup

briefdescription.para: Downcast a handle to Drawable handle.

detaileddescription.para: If handle points to an InputMethodContext the downcast produces valid handle. If not the returned handle is left uninitialized.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to an object.

simplesect.para: Handle to an Drawable or an uninitialized handle.

briefdescription.para: Downcast a handle to Gradient handle.

detaileddescription.para: If handle points to an InputMethodContext the downcast produces valid handle. If not the returned handle is left uninitialized.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to an object.

simplesect.para: Handle to an Gradient or an uninitialized handle.

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized handle to a new CanvasRenderer::LinearGradient.

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated LinearGradient

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized handle to a new CanvasRenderer::Picture.

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated Picture

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized handle to a new CanvasRenderer::RadialGradient.

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated RadialGradient

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized handle to a new CanvasRenderer::Shape.

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated Shape

briefdescription.para: Creates an initialized handle to a new CanvasRenderer.

parameterdescription.para: The viewBox of canvas.

simplesect.para: A handle to a newly allocated CanvasRenderer

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use CanvasRenderer::DrawableGroup::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Add drawable object to the DrawableGroup. This method is similar to registration.

parameterdescription.para: The drawable object.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Remove drawable object to the DrawableGroup. This method is similar to deregistration. Freeing memory is not concerned for drawables being removed.

parameterdescription.para: the drawable object.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Remove all drawable objects added to the DrawableGroup.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Constructor.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Set the transparency value.

parameterdescription.para: The transparency level [0 ~ 1.0], 0 means totally transparent, while 1 means opaque.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the transparency value.

simplesect.para: Returns the transparency level

briefdescription.para: Set the angle of rotation transformation.

parameterdescription.para: The degree value of angle.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set the scale value of scale transformation.

parameterdescription.para: The scale factor value.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set the x, y movement value of translate transformation.

parameterdescription.para: The x and y-axis movement value.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set the matrix value for affine transform.

parameterdescription.para: The 3x3 matrix value.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the bounding box of the drawable object before any transformation.

simplesect.para: The bounding box doesn't indicate the rendering region in the result but primitive region of the object.
Returns the bounding box information.

briefdescription.para: The intersection with clip drawable is determined and only the resulting pixels from own drawable are rendered.

parameterdescription.para: The clip drawable object.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: The pixels of mask drawable and own drawable are blended according to MaskType.

parameterdescription.para: The mask drawable object.
The masking type.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Constructor.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Sets the parameters of the colors of the gradient and their position.

parameterdescription.para: An array of ColorStop data structure.

simplesect.para: Result::Success when succeed.

briefdescription.para: Gets the parameters of the colors of the gradient, their position and number.

simplesect.para: Returns the colorstops list.

briefdescription.para: Set the spread.

parameterdescription.para: The current spraed type of the shape.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the spread type.

simplesect.para: Returns the current spread type of the shape.

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use CanvasRenderer::LinearGradient::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Sets the linear gradient bounds. The bounds of the linear gradient are defined as a surface constrained by two parallel lines crossing the given points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2), respectively. Both lines are perpendicular to the line linking (x1, y1) and (x2, y2).

parameterdescription.para: The first point used to determine the gradient bounds.
The second point used to determine the gradient bounds.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the linear gradient bounds.

parameterdescription.para: The first point used to determine the gradient bounds.
The second point used to determine the gradient bounds.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use CanvasRenderer::Picture::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Loads a picture data directly from a file.

parameterdescription.para: A path to the picture file.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Resize the picture content with the given size.

detaileddescription.para:
Resize the picture content while keeping the default size aspect ratio. The scaling factor is established for each of dimensions and the smaller value is applied to both of them.
size
A new size of the image in pixels.
Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the size of the image.

simplesect.para: Returns The size of the image in pixels.

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use CanvasRenderer::RadialGradient::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Sets the radial gradient bounds. The radial gradient bounds are defined as a circle centered in a given point of a given radius.

parameterdescription.para: The point of the center of the bounding circle.
The radius of the bounding circle.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the radial gradient bounds.

parameterdescription.para: The point used to determine the gradient bounds.
The radius of the bounding circle.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use CanvasRenderer::Shape::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: Append the given rectangle with rounded corner to the path. The roundedCorner arguments specify the radii of the ellipses defining the corners of the rounded rectangle.

detaileddescription.para: roundedCorner are specified in terms of width and height respectively.
If roundedCorner's values are 0, then it will draw a rectangle without rounded corner.

parameterdescription.para: size of the rectangle.
The radius of the rounded corner and should be in range [ 0 to w/2 ]

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Append a circle with given center and x,y-axis radius.

parameterdescription.para: X and Y co-ordinate of the center of the circle.
X and Y co-ordinate of radius of the circle.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (Vector2 center, float radius, float startAngle, float sweep, bool pie)
memberdef.name: AddArc

briefdescription.para: Append the arcs .

parameterdescription.para: X and Y co-ordinate of the center of the arc.
Radius of the arc.
Start angle (in degrees) where the arc begins.
The Angle measures how long the arc will be drawn.
If True, the area is created by connecting start angle point and sweep angle point of the drawn arc. If false, it doesn't.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Add a point that sets the given point as the current point, implicitly starting a new subpath and closing the previous one.

parameterdescription.para: X and Y co-ordinate of the current point.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Adds a straight line from the current position to the given end point. After the line is drawn, the current position is updated to be at the end point of the line. If no current position present, it draws a line to itself, basically * a point.

parameterdescription.para: X and Y co-ordinate of end point of the line.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Adds a cubic Bezier curve between the current position and the given end point (lineEndPoint) using the control points specified by (controlPoint1), and (controlPoint2). After the path is drawn, the current position is updated to be at the end point of the path.

parameterdescription.para: X and Y co-ordinate of 1st control point.
X and Y co-ordinate of 2nd control point.
X and Y co-ordinate of end point of the line.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Appends a given sub-path to the path. The current point value is set to the last point from the sub-path.

parameterdescription.para: The command object that contain sub-path information. (This command information is copied internally.)

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.
The interface is designed for optimal path setting if the caller has a completed path commands already.

briefdescription.para: Closes the current subpath by drawing a line to the beginning of the subpath, automatically starting a new path. The current point of the new path is (0, 0). If the subpath does not contain any points, this function does nothing.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Reset the added path(rect, circle, path, etc...) information. Color and Stroke information are keeped.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set the color to use for filling the path.

parameterdescription.para: The color value.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the color to use for filling the path.

simplesect.para: Returns The color value.

briefdescription.para: Set the gradient to use for filling the path.

parameterdescription.para: The gradient object.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the gradient to use for filling the path.

simplesect.para: Returns The gradient object.

briefdescription.para: Set the fill rule.

parameterdescription.para: The current fill rule of the shape.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the fill rule.

simplesect.para: Returns the current fill rule of the shape.

briefdescription.para: Set the stroke width to use for stroking the path.

parameterdescription.para: Stroke width to be used.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the stroke width to use for stroking the path.

simplesect.para: Returns stroke width to be used.

briefdescription.para: Set the color to use for stroking the path.

parameterdescription.para: The stroking color.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the color to use for stroking the path.

simplesect.para: Returns the stroking color.

briefdescription.para: Set the gradient to use for stroking the path.

parameterdescription.para: The gradient object.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the gradient to use for stroking the path.

simplesect.para: Returns The gradient object.

briefdescription.para: Sets the stroke dash pattern. The dash pattern is specified dash pattern.

parameterdescription.para: Lenght and a gap list.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Gets the stroke dash pattern.

simplesect.para: Returns the stroke dash pattern. The dash pattern is specified dash pattern.

briefdescription.para: Set the cap style to use for stroking the path. The cap will be used for capping the end point of a open subpath.

parameterdescription.para: Cap style to use.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the cap style to use for stroking the path.

simplesect.para: Returns the cap style.

briefdescription.para: Set the join style to use for stroking the path. The join style will be used for joining the two line segment while stroking the path.

parameterdescription.para: Join style to use.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the join style to use for stroking the path.

simplesect.para: Returns join style to use.

briefdescription.para: Creates an empty handle. Use CanvasRenderer::New() to create an initialized object.
Destructor.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

simplesect.para: A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Prepare for drawing drawables added to CanvasRenderer on inner canvas.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Add drawable object to the Canvas. This method is similar to registration. The added drawable object(shape) is drawn on the inner canvas using Commit().

parameterdescription.para: The drawable object.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Remove drawable object to the CanvasView. This method is similar to deregistration. Freeing memory is not concerned for drawables being removed.

parameterdescription.para: the drawable object.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Remove all drawable objects added to the CanvasRenderer.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Returns the rasterized texture.

simplesect.para: Returns the texture.

briefdescription.para: Draw drawables added to CanvasRenderer to inner buffer.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Returns whether the drawables added to the Canvas are changed.

simplesect.para: Returns True when drawables added to the Canvas are changed, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This is the size of the buffer in the Canvas.

parameterdescription.para: The size of canvas buffer.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This is the size of the Buffer in the Canvas.

simplesect.para: Returns The size of canvas buffer.

briefdescription.para: This is the viewbox of the Canvas.

parameterdescription.para: The size of viewbox.

simplesect.para: Returns True when it's successful. False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This is the viewbox of the Canvas.

simplesect.para: Returns The size of viewbox.

briefdescription.para: A class that holds many Drawable object. As a whole they can be transformed, their transparency can be changed.

detaileddescription.para: Used for rendering a vector primitives.
Shape is a command list for drawing one shape groups It has own path data & properties for sync/asynchronous drawing.
A class representing the radial gradient fill of the Shape object.
A class representing an image read in one of the supported formats: raw, svg, png and etc. Besides the methods inherited from the Drawable, it provides methods to load & draw images on the canvas.
A class representing the linear gradient fill of the Shape object.
An abstract class representing the gradient fill of the Shape object.
Drawable is a object class for drawing a vector primitive.

It contains the information about the gradient colors and their arrangement inside the gradient bounds. The gradients bounds are defined in the LinearGradient or RadialGradient class, depending on the type of the gradient to be used. It specifies the gradient behavior in case the area defined by the gradient bounds is smaller than the area to be filled.

Besides the class inherited from the Gradient class, it enables setting and getting the linear gradient bounds. The behavior outside the gradient bounds depends on the value specified in the spread API.
